title: cytisus balansae subsp. atlanticus
intro: Cytisus balansae subsp. atlanticus, also known as the Atlantic broom or Broom of the Atlantic, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the pea family Fabaceae. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties, which have been attributed to the presence of flavonoids and other bioactive compounds. The health benefits of Cytisus balansae subsp. atlanticus include its potential to reduce inflammation, improve wound healing, and exhibit antimicrobial activity, making it a valuable herb for topical applications. Herbal preparations of this plant can be found in the form of teas, infusions, and tinctures, often used to treat skin conditions, wounds, and minor infections.
